title: Forest and Conservation Technicians
description: Provide technical assistance regarding the conservation of soil, water, forests, or related natural resources. May compile data pertaining to size, content, condition, and other characteristics of forest tracts under the direction of foresters, or train and lead forest workers in forest propagation and fire prevention and suppression. May assist conservation scientists in managing, improving, and protecting rangelands and wildlife habitats.

tasks.text: Thin and space trees and control weeds and undergrowth, using manual tools and chemicals, or supervise workers performing these tasks.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Train and lead forest and conservation workers in seasonal activities, such as planting tree seedlings, putting out forest fires, and maintaining recreational facilities.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Provide information about, and enforce, regulations, such as those concerning environmental protection, resource utilization, fire safety, and accident prevention.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Patrol park or forest areas to protect resources and prevent damage.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Map forest tract data using digital mapping systems.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Keep records of the amount and condition of logs taken to mills.
tasks.rationale: Routine cognitive or manual task with high vulnerability to automation.

tasks.text: Manage forest protection activities, including fire control, fire crew training, and coordination of fire detection and public education programs.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Monitor activities of logging companies and contractors.
tasks.rationale: Routine cognitive or manual task with high vulnerability to automation.

tasks.text: Perform reforestation or forest renewal, including nursery and silviculture operations, site preparation, seeding and tree planting programs, cone collection, and tree improvement.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Plan and supervise construction of access routes and forest roads.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Select and mark trees for thinning or logging, drawing detailed plans that include access roads.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Supervise forest nursery operations, timber harvesting, land use activities such as livestock grazing, and disease or insect control programs.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Develop and maintain computer databases.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Inspect trees and collect samples of plants, seeds, foliage, bark, and roots to locate insect and disease damage.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Measure distances, clean sightlines, and record data to help survey crews.
tasks.rationale: Routine cognitive or manual task with high vulnerability to automation.

tasks.text: Issue fire permits, timber permits, and other forest use licenses.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Survey, measure, and map access roads and forest areas such as burns, cut-over areas, experimental plots, and timber sales sections.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Provide forestry education and general information, advice, and recommendations to woodlot owners, community organizations, and the general public.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Provide technical support to forestry research programs in areas such as tree improvement, seed orchard operations, insect and disease surveys, or experimental forestry and forest engineering research.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Conduct laboratory or field experiments with plants, animals, insects, diseases, and soils.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.
